heading: Definitions
label: 21502 Definitions
notes: References in TextThis chapter, referred to in text, was in the original “this Act”, meaning , , , known as the International Megan’s Law to Prevent Child Exploitation and Other Sexual Crimes Through Advanced Notification of Traveling Sex Offenders, which is classified principally to this chapter. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title of 2016 Act note set out under and Tables.Pub. (10)(C), is , , , which enacted chapter 209 (§ 20901 et seq.) of this title and enacted, amended, and repealed numerous other sections and notes in the Code. Sections 111, 119, and 127 of the Act were classified to sections 16911, 16919, and 16927, respectively, of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as sections 20911, 20921, and 20929, respectively, of this title. children.heading: Center
children.label: 1 Center
children.text: The term “Center” means the Angel Watch Center established pursuant to .section 21503(a) of this title

children.heading: Convicted
children.label: 2 Convicted
children.text: The term “convicted” has the meaning given the term in section 111 of the Adam Walsh Child Protection and Safety Act of 2006 ().42 U.S.C. 169111See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Covered sex offender
children.label: 3 Covered sex offender
children.text: Except as otherwise provided, the term “covered sex offender” means an individual who is a sex offender by reason of having been convicted of a sex offense against a minor.

children.heading: Destination country
children.label: 4 Destination country
children.text: The term “destination country” means a destination or transit country.

children.heading: INTERPOL
children.label: 5 INTERPOL
children.text: The term “INTERPOL” means the International Criminal Police Organization.

children.label: G
children.text: the United States Virgin Islands; and

children.label: H
children.text: to the extent provided in, and subject to the requirements of, section 127 of the Adam Walsh Child Protection and Safety Act of 2006 (), a Federally recognized Indian tribe.42 U.S.C. 169271

children.heading: Minor
children.label: 7 Minor
children.text: The term “minor” means an individual who has not attained the age of 18 years.

children.heading: National Sex Offender Registry
children.label: 8 National Sex Offender Registry
children.text: The term “National Sex Offender Registry” means the National Sex Offender Registry established by section 119 of the Adam Walsh Child Protection and Safety Act of 2006 ().42 U.S.C. 169191

children.heading: Sex offender under SORNA
children.label: 9 Sex offender under SORNA
children.text: The term “sex offender under SORNA” has the meaning given the term “sex offender” in section 111 of the Adam Walsh Child Protection and Safety Act of 2006 ().42 U.S.C. 169111

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “sex offense against a minor” means a specified offense against a minor, as defined in section 111 of the Adam Walsh Child Protection and Safety Act of 2006 ().42 U.S.C. 169111

children.heading: Other offenses
children.label: B Other offenses
children.text: The term “sex offense against a minor” includes a sex offense described in section 111(5)(A) of the Adam Walsh Child Protection and Safety Act of 2006 () that is a specified offense against a minor, as defined in paragraph (7) of such section, or an attempt or conspiracy to commit such an offense.42 U.S.C. 16911(5)(A)1

children.heading: Foreign convictions; offenses involving consensual sexual conduct
children.label: C Foreign convictions; offenses involving consensual sexual conduct
children.text: The limitations contained in subparagraphs (B) and (C) of section 111(5) of the Adam Walsh Child Protection and Safety Act of 2006 () shall apply with respect to a sex offense against a minor for purposes of this chapter to the same extent and in the same manner as such limitations apply with respect to a sex offense for purposes of the Adam Walsh Child Protection and Safety Act of 2006 [ et seq.].42 U.S.C. 16911(5)34 U.S.C. 209011
